title: Australia launches anti-dumping investigation into Turkish rebar
bodyHtml:
In a letter received from the Directorate General of Imports of the Ministry of Commerce, it is reported that an anti-dumping investigation has been initiated regarding the rebar exported to Australia from our country as well as from Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand and Vietnam and classified under Customs Tariff Statistics Positions 7214.20.00.47, 7228.30.10.70, 7228.30.90.40 and 7228.60.10.72, with reference to the letter dated 24 September 2024 of the Anti-Dumping Commission affiliated to the Australian Department of Industry, Science and Natural Resources.

In this context, the Investigation Opening Notice sent by the Australian Anti-Dumping Commission states that the relevant party's comments and exporter questionnaires have been given until 31 October 2024 to be sent by post or to investigations1@adcommission.gov.au .

In addition, exporter questionnaires, a non-confidential summary of the application, the investigation schedule and all other necessary information and documents relating to the investigation can be accessed at https://www.industry.gov.au/anti-dumpingcommission/current-cases-and-electronic-public-record-epr/655 .
